stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 4 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 8 observations with mean 62.1 % (within-trial site SD = 8.1). trial site B has 8 observations with mean 71.4 % (within-trial site SD = 7.9). trial site C has 15 observations with mean 61.2 % (within-trial site SD = 3.7). trial site D has 8 observations with mean 58.3 % (within-trial site SD = 8.1). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 8.3 and 20.9 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site A: 62.1 % (shrunk from raw 62.1). Range [62.1, 62.2] as between-trial site SD varies from 8.3 to 20.9. Pooled mean: 62.9.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 62.9 % (weighted average of 4 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/8). At σ_between=13.9: B = 0.959.
Interval [62.1, 62.2] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (62.1) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (62.9) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
